scenes.title: Open the token page from Hanji
scenes.caption: In Hanji, click “Open Notion token page” to open Notion's developer portal in a new tab.

scenes.title: Pick the workspace, create the token
scenes.caption: Choose “Access token”, then pick which Notion workspace this token applies to — a token always belongs to exactly one workspace.

scenes.title: Copy the access token
scenes.caption: Copy the ntn_… token and paste it into the token field below.

scenes.title: Connect it to your pages
scenes.caption: In Notion, open each top-level page → ··· → Connections → add this connection. Subpages are included automatically — then come back and scan.

scenes.title: Paste the token into Hanji
scenes.caption: Back in Hanji, paste the token you copied into the “Notion API token” field.

scenes.title: Choose what to import
scenes.caption: Import the entire workspace, or pick “Specific pages” to bring in only the pages and databases you select.

mock.inkIntroTitle: Import with a Notion API token
mock.inkIntroDesc: For local hosting, pasting a token you create in Notion is the most reliable setup.
mock.scopeAllDesc: Everything the connection can access. Database relations between pages stay intact.
mock.scopeSomeDesc: Scan accessible top-level items, then import only the pages/databases you select.
mock.listSubtitle: Create and manage Notion's public and private API connections.
mock.capabilities: Read content, update and insert content, and read user information
mock.modalDesc: Create a connection to authenticate apps, agents, and workflows with Notion. Choose a single-workspace or multi-workspace scope.
mock.accessTokenDesc: A static, workspace-scoped API token shared between collaborators, with its own capability settings. Limited to 1 workspace and cannot be listed on the Marketplace.
mock.oauthDesc: User-scoped OAuth 2.0; permissions are limited to the logged-in user. Supports multiple workspaces.
mock.wsDesc: This token applies to the selected workspace.
mock.terms: By creating a connection you agree to Notion's developer terms.
mock.tokenSectionDesc: Use this token to authenticate API requests for this workspace. Anyone with the token can act on the connection's behalf, so keep it secret.
